reviews.review_description: WHAT THE FRICK BRO WHY DO U HAVE TO PAY MONEY TO MAKE A SECOND PROGECT >:(  still a good game tho
Most modern applications that a designer could find. Gives you the space to learn, absorb, and experience creativity from start to finish. Looking forward to see what more is to come !
Works great.  The future of fully immersive design can be seen right here!

ShapesXR is the perfect tool for any designer passionate about virtual reality, immersive design, or creating interactive experiences. 

It's surprisingly intuitive from the start, allowing you to quickly and easily create interactive prototypes. You can even work with your team within the same environment.
Plus, they continue to add new features to further improve the app.

I definitely recommend it!

reviews.review_title: A nice tool to experiment with!
reviews.review_description: ShapesXR provides me a space to experiment with personal 3D and Figma assets in a virtual space!

You can really do all what you want. For example I use it for home design, thanks to the very good mixed reality support. You can literally create every shape you need, texturing it, painting it. You can place furniture in your irl room and see if it fit well in the place. And so many other features.

The online support is very nice and reactive.

I highly recommend this app

ShapesXR makes it easy to map out ideas directly in 3D, iterate quickly, and share concepts with teammates in real time. The spatial sketching tools are intuitive, and the collaborative features help bridge the gap between design and development seamlessly.
Not the easiest tutorial ever kind of nerdy

I have been learning how to design, and Shapes XR really has helped exponentially with being able to create a storyboard/ conceptual mock up of the product I’m working on. I don’t know how to code (yet) and this is opening doors for me and many other novice developers.
This game is fun
They meet expectations of me about inspecting my 3d design in MR environment. Unfortunately there is not such kind of apps nowadays. This is one of the most succesful one.

I’m a student that’s working on a MR concept for my masters thesis and ShapesXR has been a life saver. It’s very intuitive and its a great tool to try in a fast and easy way UX elements.

I recommend it to everyone that wants to do a semi-functional prototype before jumping in a more real and totally functional project.

Awesome, lightweight tool for prototyping ideas in XR. Would love to see more file types supported in the future and more interactions. Would also love features for porting to Unity or Unreal in the future for development.

reviews.review_title: Great Tool for Beginners in XR Design
reviews.review_description: As someone new to XR design, I’m very impressed with how intuitive and beginner-friendly ShapesXR is. It made diving into prototyping way less intimidating and actually fun. The learning curve felt super manageable thanks to their solid tutorials, and the features are really well thought out for fast iteration and collaboration. Plus, the customer support team is fast, friendly, and genuinely helpful. Highly recommend it to anyone looking to break into XR design.

As a student working on my bachelor's thesis, ShapesXR has been an incredible tool for UX testing in virtual reality. The intuitive interface, collaborative features, and flexibility of the platform made it easy to design and test immersive environments.

What really stood out was the support from the team—they were quick to respond. It's rare to find a tool that’s both powerful and accessible, especially for academic projects.

Highly recommend ShapesXR for anyone working in spatial design, prototyping, or VR-based research!

Amazing creative tool! Most of my time on the Quest is in ShapesXR, exploring ideas in location-based MR installation art. The pass-through engagement is solid and this newest update seems substantial. Can’t wait to jump back in.
I'm truly impressed with ShapesXR. When I've reached out via email with questions, the team has responded quickly, helpfully and friendly.

This is probably the laziest, most worthless app design I have ever seen in my life, complete with a useless meta avatar that screams at top volume while a text box is the one that actually teaches you how to paint the same object six different times in a row. Truly a disgusting attempt at a tutorial for what should be extremely intuitive software. I suppose when you make god tools for children that's bound to happen, but it's still extremely disappointing that such little effort went into anything beyond the logo for this app. I couldn't complete the tutorial before I got so bored and irritated I just went to one of ten different spatial design apps that function better, with better tutorials and more assets. This one can stay in the corporate garbage where it belongs.

As a complete beginner using ShapesXR for a school project, I was amazed at how easy it is to use. The interface is intuitive, and it guides you through the process, making VR design feel approachable.

One thing I’d improve is adding a warning when too many objects are placed in a space, as it can cause rendering issues. Thx

reviews.review_title: Meta Quest and ShapesXR: Transforming VR
reviews.review_description:
My VR classes are using the Meta Quest 2 and 3 to explore immersive learning, and the ShapesXR app has been a game-changer. This innovative tool has empowered students to dive into 3D design and prototyping in ways we couldn’t have imagined before.  Engagement is through the roof.

ShapesXR's intuitive interface makes it easy for students to create complex 3D models and environments collaboratively, fostering creativity and problem-solving skills. The real-time interaction and ability to iterate on designs within a shared virtual space are unlike anything traditional tools offer.

Meta Quest’s lightweight design and wireless functionality make it perfect for classroom use. Students can jump into their projects seamlessly, without worrying about cumbersome setups. The combination of this cutting-edge hardware and ShapesXR’s capabilities has unlocked new levels of engagement and innovation in our learning environment.

My students are thriving in this dynamic VR workspace, and we’re excited to see how it continues to inspire their creativity and design skills. ShapesXR on Meta Quest is a must-have for anyone looking to revolutionize their approach to education and 3D design!

Just started using the Meta Quest 3 and was looking for a tool for prototyping for an interaction design course at university without having to know programming beforehand. ShapesXR was perfect since I was instantly able to make easy and intuitive prototypes. A big plus is being able to link Figma designs directly into your environment. Overall a very good app!
ShapesXR is an amazing tool for quick prototyping. The app is very intuitive and well designed. Figma Plugin is just a great bonus.

ShapesXR has completely transformed my design process. As an information design student, I was looking for a tool that would allow me to bring my ideas to life in a more immersive way. ShapesXR has been a game-changer. The intuitive interface makes it a breeze to create and iterate on designs, and the collaborative features are fantastic for brainstorming with my team. It's like having a virtual whiteboard that you can walk around in. I've been using it for everything from prototyping user interfaces to visualizing data structures. It's a must-have for any designer looking to push the boundaries of their creativity.

reviews.review_title: A unique prototyping tool for VR
reviews.review_description: We have been using ShapesXR to teach our students the design process in VR, and it has been extremely useful. The ability to quickly design an environment and test it out without ever leaving VR is something that I have never seen outside of this application. ShapesXR has a healthy library of useful assets as well as the very useful ability to import assets. My only suggestion is that I wish we could add more fleshed out interactions for more interesting and interactive stages.

reviews.review_title: Collaborative VR for All Skill Levels
reviews.review_description: ShapesXR has become an invaluable tool in my classroom for introducing students to the exciting possibilities of collaborative VR design. Its intuitive interface and user-friendly features make it easy to dive in and start creating together. Whether they're brainstorming ideas, building 3D models, or developing interactive storyboards, ShapesXR provides a dynamic and engaging platform for collaborative learning. The ability to seamlessly transition between desktop and VR environments further enhances its versatility, catering to different learning styles and preferences. ShapesXR has truly revolutionized the way I teach collaborative VR design, and I highly recommend it to any educator looking to inspire and engage their students.

Nothing compares to Shapes XR. The moment I signed in and followed the tutorials and learned the basics I was off creating my prototype. With help from Jordan, I was able to take advantage of the professional plan that suits all necessities for continued creativity. Thank you Meta for bringing us one step closer to developing the future of Mixed reality.

This is an excellent tool for prototying products and experiences in XR.
The interface is simple and straightforward to pick up, it has all the right functionalities to allow visualization of ideas and collaborative design. The new ShapesXR 2.0 has brought this already excellent application to new levels, interactions and attach points really allow to make VR prototypes come to life.

reviews.review_title: So Intuitive & fast for rapid prototypes
reviews.review_description: Shapes XR has truly transformed my workflow making rapid prototyping so intuitive as sketching your ideas into the space around you. The pass-through remote is in credible! It opens up opportunities for us XR designers and  creative directors to explore Mixed Reality concepts with real environments.

developer_response.body:
Many designers do not understand how important it is to design at scale in 3D, especially for Mixed Reality apps!
Thanks for pointing that out so clearly

reviews.review_description: I can't wait to play the game in 2 hour

developer_response.body: We hope you had a creative blast 😉

reviews.review_description:
https://www.oculus.com/referrals/link/shaundashepp/

If you have not got a oculus yet but plan on it use this code and you will get $30 bucks for free and you can use that money to buy games when you get your oculus. It will also work if you got your headset less then 7 days ago.

reviews.review_title: Thank you for this brilliant App
reviews.review_description:
Thank you  #ShapesXR. It's a brilliant app which helped us a lot in developing the #UX for the our NoPhob mixed reality app. Communicating ideas while being in a 3D space is key to defining how the end user will experience the features and UI in a VR Setup. Using ShapesXR at the start of our design journey we were able to map out our ideas smoothly and most importantly effectively.

Excellent for collaboration while in different locations or even in the same room. One can quickly prototype ideas and define user journey.

developer_response.body: ShapesXR is indeed great for MR design and initial ideation. Glad to hear it made a difference

reviews.review_description: I have worked in VR/AR/XR for more than 12 years. I have used various tools to develop experiences. Never have I encountered a more intuitive prototyping tool for virtual and mixed reality.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much. We have made easy of use one of our top priorities and we will keep working to lower the entry barrier and make 3D creation accessible for everyone

reviews.review_title: Can’t play without eye tracking enabled
reviews.review_description: Can’t play without eye tracking enabled on Quest Pro. Why is this a requirement for this app? Please make it optional developers.

developer_response.body: You are 100% right and we just made eye-tracking completely optional. It would be very appreciated if you could reconsider the review 🙏

reviews.review_title: It won’t let me the Game
reviews.review_description:
Please fix
It

developer_response.body:
Hi Miguel,
thank you for your note. We are very sorry to hear bout that.
All Quest Pro-ready apps moved to v46 of the headset firmware and it seems not everyone got the update. Could you please make sure that your Quest is on v46, not v44? The v44 prevents the app from launching.
You can check your headset updates in settings and go for it. 
Please let us know if this helped.

reviews.review_description:
I like fun only
     





Bye…

developer_response.body: Sad to hear you didn't find in ShapesXR what you were expecting. Maybe you can join one of the multiplayer VR onboardings on our website (www.shapesxr.com) with one of our instructors. That might help 😉

reviews.review_title: Less App for Much More Money
reviews.review_description: Well the app is free unless you want to actually use it, in which case they expect you to pay $10 a month. Gravity sketch is free, multi-brush is free, and neither has a monthly charge. If there's a reason to pay $10 a month for this app they've done no work to let you know what it is. Skip this and get gravity sketch.

developer_response.body: Hi Bryan, maybe you are right and we haven't done the BEST job at communicating this within the app. The FREE version of ShapesXR is fully featured and you can do ANYTHING that is possible also in the Pro version. The only difference at the moment is in the number of spaces you can create and the cloud storage you have available. Of course, if you are looking for a 3D modeling apps, Gravity Sketch is one of the best products on the market (we love it as well 😉), but if you want a very accessible way to prototype your AR and VR apps or assemble quickly scenes in VR we strongly believe that ShapesXR gives the opportunity to start right now for FREE with a top-class real-time co-creation experience. Invite some of your friends and let us know how it goes. Looking forward to hear your feedback.

reviews.review_description:
I used this to prototype a TV show with 3 different rooms and a big stage in just a few hours. You can upload  your own models and images and place it anywhere in the scene. 
With the play mode you guide other people through the scenes it and gather feedback of your work. Actually the best productivity app on the Quest right now!

developer_response.body: This is so inspiring! Thank you very much kaimys for the kind words. This is exactly how we envisioned ShapesXR to be used: quick and accessible design, team review and overall a whole new level of productivity unlocked in VR.

reviews.review_title: Utilizes the full potential of VR
reviews.review_description:
Im a student and have been using Shapes XR to ideate, visualize and communicate my industrial design projects with great extend. Importing .obj-files (from Fusion 360 in my case) and setting up a scene is super easy and so is creating immersive storyboards with the stages feature. 

Im currently using ShapesXR to prototype an AR User Interface and it's a massive time saver. Passthrough mode paired with the MRTK templates comes close to actually implementing the ui in unity. Great news that they are working on interactivity which will bring them even closer. 

Impressive work! Im looking forward to future updates.

developer_response.body:
From industrial design to AR that's quite impressive.
Great to see you are using Shapes on so many different projects!

reviews.review_description:
The Pros: It's a FREE introduction to working in 3D.

The Cons: Unless you work in 3D already, it's hard to master. 

Coming from 2D graphics, I'm (wrongly) expecting it to work like Adobe software. Where are the points and handles? Why can I color a stroke, but not fill a space I've created? Where is the pen tool? Why is everything grouped from the start? And where are the layers?! I may be incorrect to expect all of the above, but layers? There must be layers! How can there not be layers?! 

If you have zero experience in 3D, this isn't as intuitive as some reviewers would have you believe. In addition, you must be super skilled with your controllers, because pointing and grabbing individual items is like trying to catch bubbles. 

I tried Gravity Sketch as well and found this app to have better out-of-the-gate capabilities, but there must be more instruction somewhere (other than the initial tutorial - which was great, by the way) for newbies like me! To be honest, I can't even find the exit door for the app. I have to open my Oculus menu to quit the experience. 

Bottom line: there is so much potential here. I think it's going to be a great tool to bring me up to speed with working in 3D, because I have a fantastic idea for an app, but I need to learn how to create a prototype to pitch it. (That's the whole reason I bought my Oculus in the first place.) But it is NOT for beginners. Until I find the more in-depth tutorials to fully utilize it, it will be a source of frustration, with a steeper-than-expected learning curve.

(I will happily update this review once I figure out what I'm doing. In the meantime, I hope I can prepare others for the frustration that awaits them.)

developer_response.body:
Hi FlynnTwin
Let us start by saying that we are very happy you decided to try ShapesXR and that you see it playing an important role in your design and ideation process. Moving from creating in 2D to 3D can be challenging so sit back relax and let us help you the best we can.
 

1. First of all I invite you to join one of our onboarding sessions when one of our instructors can answer your questions, teach you the basics and even share some advanced tips and tricks. You can book one at the time that works for you directly on www.shapesxr.com by clicking the big button that says "Schedule Onboarding"
 

2. We have tooltips to help you beyond the initial tutorial. By pointing your dominant hand to a specific tool you will see a question mark appearing on the B or Y button. By pressing that button a video or 3D tooltip will appear to explain what the tool does.
 

3. We do have some sort of "Layers". We call them "stages" and you can access them by flicking the thumbstick up on your non dominant hand. These are used like frames ina storyboard and allows you to simulate interactions or user flows. You can also copy paste objects between stages while maintaining the exact position...(we can show you that during the onboarding session or you can also check out our learn section on the website).
 

4.We do have some analogue to the pen tool but that is very dependent on what you would like to do with it 😅. You can use the text tool, you have the stroke tool and some of the brushes are also volumetric and you can use them to create soft or hard hull shapes (even precisely if you toggle the grid on).
 

5. We are not sure what is that was grouped form the start. You can always ungroup object by pressing the A or X button on your dominant hand.
 

6. We already have a system that "simulates" your beloved Adobe features 😉. You can snap objects to a surface, Toggle the gizmo to move or stretch an object precisely, use the handles to align objects etc. There is a lot going (oh and did I mention we organize onboarding sessions ?😅 ). We are working on a more streamlined system that would make this process more streamlined so stay tuned.
 

We sincerely hope this is going to relieve some of that frustration and do not hesitate to reach out to use directly at hey@shapesxr.com for any issue or specific question.

reviews.review_description: I made my first 3D presentation! Feels so cool. Excited to try it out with team

developer_response.body: Let us know how it goes! And don't forget that you can always use the web panel at shapes.app to create web link and share the space with others directly on the browser 😉
